deep_dive.title: Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising for Robust Multi-view 3D Reconstruction
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-view 3D reconstruction has achieved remarkable progress with the advent of feed-forward 3D reconstruction models. However, these models are typically trained and evaluated under ideal, degradation-free imaging conditions, whereas real-world observations often contain degradations that differ significantly from such settings. Improving robustness for multi-view 3D reconstruction under degraded conditions therefore remains an important challenge. We present Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising (GARD), a novel framework that performs diffusion-based multi-view restoration directly in the feature space of a feed-forward 3D reconstruction model. This design exploits the geometry-aware feature representations of the 3D reconstructor to effectively recover accurate scene geometry. Furthermore, by employing an additional RGB image decoder, the refined representations can also be used to restore high-quality RGB images, thereby enabling the simultaneous recovery of 3D scene geometry and high-quality imagery. Comprehensive experiments on the Depth Anything 3 (DA3) benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed GARD framework.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion-based denoising of feed-forward 3D reconstruction features
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散的前馈3D重建特征去噪方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于扩散的前馈3D重建特征去噪方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散的前馈3D重建特征去噪方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising (GARD) to perform diffusion-based restoration in the feature space of feed-forward 3D reconstruction models, improving robustness under real-world degradations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对前馈3D重建模型在真实退化条件下鲁棒性不足的问题，提出几何感知表征去噪（GARD）框架。该框架直接在重建模型的几何感知特征空间上进行扩散去噪，无需修改模型本身。实验表明，GARD在多种退化条件下显著提升重建质量，且对原始干净输入无副作用。该工作推动了前馈3D重建在真实场景中的应用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对前馈3D重建模型在真实退化条件下鲁棒性不足的问题，提出几何感知表征去噪（GARD）框架。该框架直接在重建模型的几何感知特征空间上进行扩散去噪，无需修改模型本身。实验表明，GARD在多种退化条件下显著提升重建质量，且对原始干净输入无副作用。该工作推动了前馈3D重建在真实场景中的应用。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to feed forward 3D重建, especially work that connects or combines: feed-forward neural networks for 3D reconstruction; direct mapping from pixels to 3D geometry using feed-forward networks; state of the art feed forward 3D reconstruction methods; feed forward vs optimization based 3D reconstruction; efficient large scale 3D reconstruction models.

deep_dive.title: $R^3$: 3D Reconstruction via Relative Regression
deep_dive.abstract: Recent feed-forward geometry foundation models have demonstrated impressive generalization by recovering depth and poses in a single forward pass. However, these models are typically constrained by a global coordinate frame assumption. This dependency becomes a significant bottleneck for long-context and streaming reconstruction, as it forces the network to maintain an arbitrary temporal origin and handle translation magnitudes that grow unbounded over time. Our solution, which we call $R^3$, employs relative regression. We employ a lightweight MLP to predict confidence-weighted relative constraints. These confidences serve as a unified anchor: weighting losses during training and guiding pose aggregation during inference. $R^3$ supports both full-context offline reconstruction and causal, bounded-memory streaming. Our evaluation in both offline and streaming settings validates the effectiveness of our relative mechanism. Project page: https://kevinxu02.github.io/r3-site
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过相对回归的前馈3D重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过相对回归的前馈3D重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过相对回归的前馈3D重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Relative regression overcomes the global coordinate frame limitation of feed-forward geometry foundation models, enabling long-context reconstruction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对前馈几何基础模型依赖全局坐标框架的瓶颈，提出相对回归方法，通过轻量MLP预测相对约束，支持流式和大规模重建，展示了前馈方法在长场景下的优越性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对前馈几何基础模型依赖全局坐标框架的瓶颈，提出相对回归方法，通过轻量MLP预测相对约束，支持流式和大规模重建，展示了前馈方法在长场景下的优越性。

deep_dive.title: FoundObj: Self-supervised Foundation Models as Rewards for Label-free 3D Object Segmentation
deep_dive.abstract: We address the challenging task of 3D object segmentation in complex scene point clouds without relying on any scene-level human annotations during training. Existing methods are typically constrained to identifying simple objects, primarily due to insufficient object priors in the learning process. In this paper, we present FoundObj, a novel framework featuring a superpoint-based object discovery agent that incrementally merges suitable neighboring superpoints, guided by our innovative semantic and geometric reward modules. These modules synergistically leverage semantic and geometric priors from self-supervised 2D/3D foundation models, providing complementary feedback to the object discovery agent and enabling robust identification of multi-class objects through reinforcement learning. Extensive experiments on diverse benchmarks demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms existing baselines. Notably, our method exhibits strong generalization in zero-shot and long-tail scenarios, underscoring its potential for scalable, label-free 3D object segmentation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: object instance segmentation in 3D point clouds
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FoundObj, a framework for label-free 3D object segmentation in scene point clouds. It uses a superpoint-based discovery agent with semantic and geometric reward modules leveraging self-supervised 2D/3D foundation models to guide merging, enabling robust identification of complex objects without scene-level annotations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 无标注3D物体分割任务中，现有方法限于简单物体。本文提出FoundObj框架，采用超点发现代理逐步合并邻近超点，并由语义和几何奖励模块引导。这些奖励模块利用自监督2D/3D基础模型的先验知识提供互补反馈。在ScanNet等数据上，该方法无需任何场景级标注即可分割出复杂物体，性能接近全监督方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 无标注3D物体分割任务中，现有方法限于简单物体。本文提出FoundObj框架，采用超点发现代理逐步合并邻近超点，并由语义和几何奖励模块引导。这些奖励模块利用自监督2D/3D基础模型的先验知识提供互补反馈。在ScanNet等数据上，该方法无需任何场景级标注即可分割出复杂物体，性能接近全监督方法。

deep_dive.title: Unified Panoramic Geometry Estimation via Multi-View Foundation Models
deep_dive.abstract: Geometry estimation from perspective images has greatly advanced, maturing to the point where off-the-shelf foundation models are able to reconstruct 3D scene structure not only from multi-view imagery, but even from a single view. A natural extension is 3D reconstruction from panoramas, with the exciting prospect of recovering a full 360-degree scene from a single panoramic image. In this work, we introduce PaGeR (Panoramic Geometry Reconstruction), a framework to lift powerful 3D foundation models designed for perspective imagery to the panorama domain. Our strategy is to start from a pre-trained transformer for 3D reconstruction and turn it into a unified high-performance model that predicts scale-invariant depth, metric depth, surface normals, and sky masks from both perspective and omnidirectional images, in a single forward pass. By keeping architectural changes to a minimum and mixing perspective and panoramic images during training, PaGeR retains the rich 3D prior of the underlying foundation model while learning to also estimate geometrically consistent 360-degree scenes from single panoramas. We extensively test our method in both indoor and outdoor environments and find that it delivers state-of-the-art performance and excellent zero-shot performance across a wide range of scenes.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: pre-trained transformer for 3D reconstruction adapted to panoramas
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将预训练Transformer用于3D重建并适配到全景领域
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将预训练Transformer用于3D重建并适配到全景领域
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将预训练Transformer用于3D重建并适配到全景领域
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PaGeR lifts perspective 3D foundation models to panoramas for unified depth and normal estimation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有3D基础模型多用于透视图像，全景3D重建具有单图360度场景恢复的潜力。提出PaGeR，将预训练的3D重建Transformer提升到全景领域，统一预测尺度不变深度、公制深度和表面法线。实验表明该方法在全景数据集上达到最佳效果。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有3D基础模型多用于透视图像，全景3D重建具有单图360度场景恢复的潜力。提出PaGeR，将预训练的3D重建Transformer提升到全景领域，统一预测尺度不变深度、公制深度和表面法线。实验表明该方法在全景数据集上达到最佳效果。

deep_dive.title: Feedforward 3D Editing Learns from Semantic-Part Transformation
deep_dive.abstract: 3D editing is a fundamental capability for scalable 3D content creation. While image editing has rapidly evolved toward large-scale feedforward generative paradigms, 3D AI generation remains dominated by training-free editing pipelines. A central challenge of feedforward 3D editing lies in the lack of high-quality paired supervision. Editable 3D assets require simultaneous preservation of geometry, multi-view consistency, structural coherence, and localized edit controllability. Existing 3D editing datasets often rely on independently generated assets, image-mediated reconstruction or narrow edit taxonomies, leading to inaccurate localization, weak preservation, blurred edit boundaries, and limited semantic consistency. In this work, we introduce a new perspective: scalable feedforward 3D editing should be learned from semantic-part transformations. Based on this insight, we propose Pxform, a high-quality 3D editing dataset with over 100K consistent before/after editing pairs across seven edit types. Instead of treating objects as unstructured shapes, our pipeline grounds edits directly in semantic 3D parts. Built upon Pxform, we further propose PartFlow, a feedforward 3D editing network that injects source-aware latent control into pretrained 3D generative priors. PartFlow introduces mask-aware velocity preservation and render-space consistency supervision to jointly improve edit fidelity and source preservation, while requiring no 3D edit mask during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that high-quality semantic-part supervision substantially improves scalable 3D editing, enabling PartFlow to achieve state-of-the-art performance on both geometric and appearance editing benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于语义部件变换的前馈式3D编辑
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于语义部件变换的前馈式3D编辑
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于语义部件变换的前馈式3D编辑
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a feedforward 3D editing framework that learns from semantic-part transformations, addressing lack of paired supervision in 3D editing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的3D编辑方法多采用无需训练的流水线，而前馈式3D编辑面临高质量配对数据缺乏的问题。本文提出一种从语义部件变换中学习的前馈式3D编辑方法，通过新的视角和数据集构建，实现了几何保持、多视角一致和局部编辑可控。实验证明该方法在编辑质量和效率上均优于现有方法，为前馈式3D内容创建提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有的3D编辑方法多采用无需训练的流水线，而前馈式3D编辑面临高质量配对数据缺乏的问题。本文提出一种从语义部件变换中学习的前馈式3D编辑方法，通过新的视角和数据集构建，实现了几何保持、多视角一致和局部编辑可控。实验证明该方法在编辑质量和效率上均优于现有方法，为前馈式3D内容创建提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: TrackRef3D: Multi-View Consistent Track-then-Label for Open-World Referring Segmentation in 3D Gaussian Splatting
deep_dive.abstract: Referring 3D Gaussian Splatting (R3DGS), which utilizes natural language for 3D object segmentation, has emerged as a crucial capability for embodied AI. However, existing methods typically rely on expensive per-scene manual annotation and per-view pseudo mask generation, which suffer from multi-view inconsistency and poor generalization to varying query specificities. To address this, we present TrackRef3D, a fully automatic pipeline that achieves open-world referring segmentation in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) without manual annotation by introducing a multi-view consistent track-then-label paradigm that fundamentally decouples object discovery from semantic grounding. Specifically, we propose a Trajectory-Aware Semantic Consensus Module (TSCM) which aggregates cross-view predictions via synonymous clustering and trajectory-aware voting to establish a canonical semantic identity, thereby ensuring multi-view consistency. Furthermore, we employ a visibility-aware description generation strategy to mitigate ambiguity and propose a Hybrid Training Strategy (HTS) that jointly optimizes coarse category semantics and fine-grained referential cues to ensure robustness under varying query specificities using a multi-positive contrastive objective. Extensive experiments on benchmarks demonstrate that TrackRef3D achieves state-of-the-art performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: 3D object segmentation with natural language, addressing part-level semantic labeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用自然语言进行3D对象分割，实现部件级语义标注
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用自然语言进行3D对象分割，实现部件级语义标注
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用自然语言进行3D对象分割，实现部件级语义标注
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TrackRef3D achieves open-world referring segmentation in 3D Gaussian Splatting without manual annotation via a track-then-label paradigm that decouples object discovery from semantic grounding, enabling multi-view consistent and query-general segmentation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有3D指代分割方法依赖人工标注和多视图不一致的问题，本文提出TrackRef3D，一种全自动管道，通过轨迹-标签范式解耦对象发现与语义定位，在3D高斯散射中实现开放世界指代分割。该方法无需人工标注，具备多视图一致性和对多样化查询的泛化能力，为3D部件分割任务提供了高效、灵活的解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有3D指代分割方法依赖人工标注和多视图不一致的问题，本文提出TrackRef3D，一种全自动管道，通过轨迹-标签范式解耦对象发现与语义定位，在3D高斯散射中实现开放世界指代分割。该方法无需人工标注，具备多视图一致性和对多样化查询的泛化能力，为3D部件分割任务提供了高效、灵活的解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Unified Panoramic Geometry Estimation via Multi-View Foundation Models
deep_dive.abstract: Geometry estimation from perspective images has greatly advanced, maturing to the point where off-the-shelf foundation models are able to reconstruct 3D scene structure not only from multi-view imagery, but even from a single view. A natural extension is 3D reconstruction from panoramas, with the exciting prospect of recovering a full 360-degree scene from a single panoramic image. In this work, we introduce PaGeR (Panoramic Geometry Reconstruction), a framework to lift powerful 3D foundation models designed for perspective imagery to the panorama domain. Our strategy is to start from a pre-trained transformer for 3D reconstruction and turn it into a unified high-performance model that predicts scale-invariant depth, metric depth, surface normals, and sky masks from both perspective and omnidirectional images, in a single forward pass. By keeping architectural changes to a minimum and mixing perspective and panoramic images during training, PaGeR retains the rich 3D prior of the underlying foundation model while learning to also estimate geometrically consistent 360-degree scenes from single panoramas. We extensively test our method in both indoor and outdoor environments and find that it delivers state-of-the-art performance and excellent zero-shot performance across a wide range of scenes. Code, data and models are available $\href{https://github.com/prs-eth/PaGeR}{\text{here}}$.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Feed-forward transformer for 3D reconstruction from panoramas
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PaGeR lifts a pre-trained feed-forward transformer for 3D reconstruction to the panorama domain, enabling unified prediction of depth, normals, and geometry from a single panoramic image.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 全景3D重建面临缺乏专门模型的问题。本文提出PaGeR，将预训练的前馈3D重建Transformer扩展到全景领域，实现从单张全景图像预测尺度不变深度、公制深度、表面法向等几何信息。该方法在多个全景数据集上取得了最先进的性能，展示了前馈模型在全景重建中的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 全景3D重建面临缺乏专门模型的问题。本文提出PaGeR，将预训练的前馈3D重建Transformer扩展到全景领域，实现从单张全景图像预测尺度不变深度、公制深度、表面法向等几何信息。该方法在多个全景数据集上取得了最先进的性能，展示了前馈模型在全景重建中的有效性。

deep_dive.title: Joint 2D-3D Segmentation and Association in Street-level Imaging
deep_dive.abstract: Accurate interpretation of street-level imagery is essential for large-scale urban mapping and the creation of Spatial Digital Twin (SDT) environments. This work presents a unified framework for joint 2D-3D segmentation and association that integrates visual semantics with multi-view geometric reasoning. Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on sequential frames for temporal tracking, our method leverages zero-shot detection and segmentation together with structure-from-motion reconstruction to establish stable cross-view correspondences. A 3D-driven association mechanism replaces traditional 2D multi-object tracking, using geometric consistency to guide identity preservation across wide-baseline viewpoints and varying imaging conditions. By combining 2D texture cues with global 3D context, the proposed pipeline is well-suited for scalable street-level processing and can be used for a variety of object types. Experiments demonstrate substantially improved coverage of ground-truth sequences and more robust identity retention compared to state-of-the-art 2D-only tracking methods, achieving a 22% performance gain in challenging urban scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: joint 2D-3D segmentation and association for street-level imagery
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联合2D-3D分割与关联的街道级成像方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联合2D-3D分割与关联的街道级成像方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联合2D-3D分割与关联的街道级成像方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a unified framework for joint 2D-3D segmentation and association using zero-shot detection and SfM reconstruction, enabling stable cross-view correspondences for urban mapping.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对街道级成像中的2D-3D分割与关联问题，提出一个统一框架。该框架利用零样本检测与分割结合运动恢复结构重建，建立稳定的跨视点对应。通过3D驱动的关联机制替代传统2D多目标跟踪，实现宽基线下的身份保持。实验表明该方法在城市地图构建中具有高效性和鲁棒性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对街道级成像中的2D-3D分割与关联问题，提出一个统一框架。该框架利用零样本检测与分割结合运动恢复结构重建，建立稳定的跨视点对应。通过3D驱动的关联机制替代传统2D多目标跟踪，实现宽基线下的身份保持。实验表明该方法在城市地图构建中具有高效性和鲁棒性。

quick_skim.title: DinoComplete: 3D Shape Completion with Distilled Semantic Priors and State Space Models
quick_skim.abstract: 3D shape completion from partial scans remains challenging for unseen categories and noisy real-world observations, where geometry alone is often insufficient for inferring missing structure. We present DinoComplete, a deterministic and efficient shape completion framework that augments geometric reconstruction with voxel-aligned semantic priors distilled from DINO features. First, we construct multi-view DINO feature volumes aligned with ShapeNet data and train a student network to predict dense semantic features directly from incomplete shapes. These predicted features capture global structure and part-aware semantic context while remaining aligned with the underlying geometry. We then integrate these distilled features into a completion network, where geometric and semantic voxel representations are fused through voxel state-space modeling. To enable efficient long-range reasoning without sacrificing resolution, we introduce a multi-scale voxel Mamba module that refines the fused features by combining full-grid and chunk-wise sequence modeling. Experiments on unseen ShapeNet categories and ScanNet objects show that DinoComplete achieves stronger completion quality than prior deterministic and generative based completion methods while using fewer parameters, requiring lower memory, and achieving faster inference. Our results demonstrate that distilling semantic priors from visual foundation models improves generalization and robustness in 3D shape completion.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward 3D shape completion with semantic priors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用蒸馏语义先验的前馈3D形状补全
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用蒸馏语义先验的前馈3D形状补全
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用蒸馏语义先验的前馈3D形状补全
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DinoComplete uses distilled semantic priors from DINO and state space models for deterministic 3D shape completion from partial scans, achieving robust performance on unseen categories.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 3D形状补全在不完整扫描和未知类别上仍具挑战，仅几何信息不足。提出DinoComplete，利用从DINO蒸馏的语义先验和状态空间模型，构建多视图DINO特征体积，训练学生网络从不完整形状预测密集语义特征并集成到补全框架。在ShapeNet等数据集上展示了鲁棒性和高效性，证明语义先验有效提升补全质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 3D形状补全在不完整扫描和未知类别上仍具挑战，仅几何信息不足。提出DinoComplete，利用从DINO蒸馏的语义先验和状态空间模型，构建多视图DINO特征体积，训练学生网络从不完整形状预测密集语义特征并集成到补全框架。在ShapeNet等数据集上展示了鲁棒性和高效性，证明语义先验有效提升补全质量。

quick_skim.title: MuNet: A Mutualistic Network for Joint 3D Human Mesh Recovery and 3D Clothed Human Reconstruction from Single Images
quick_skim.abstract: 3D human mesh recovery and 3D clothed human reconstruction are inherently related, yet they have long been studied in isolation, thereby overlooking the potential gains of joint optimization. To overcome this limitation, we propose to address these two tasks within a unified framework, which allows their mutual dependencies to be effectively exploited. Building on this idea, we propose MuNet, a mutualistic network for joint 3D human mesh recovery and 3D clothed human reconstruction from single images. First, we adopt 2-manifold graphs as a unified representation for all 3D models, enabling consistent modeling across 3D human mesh recovery and clothed human reconstruction. Second, we design an end-to-end graph convolutional network that progressively deforms an initial graph into a 3D human mesh and refines it into a detailed 3D clothed human model. Third, we introduce a mutualistic mechanism that allows reciprocal interaction between the two tasks {during training}, where 3D human mesh recovery provides guidance for 3D clothed human reconstruction, and reconstruction feedback refines the 3D human mesh recovery. We extensively evaluate MuNet on six benchmark datasets for 3D human mesh recovery and 3D clothed human reconstruction, including Human3.6M, 3DPW, MPI-INF-3DHP, THuman2.0, CAPE, and RenderPeople. Experimental results demonstrate that MuNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on both tasks across all datasets. The code of MuNet is released for research purposes at https://github.com/starVisionTeam/MuNet.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: joint 3D human mesh recovery and clothed human reconstruction from single images
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从单张图像联合恢复3D人体网格和穿衣人体重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从单张图像联合恢复3D人体网格和穿衣人体重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从单张图像联合恢复3D人体网格和穿衣人体重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MuNet jointly optimizes 3D human mesh recovery and clothed human reconstruction from a single image using a unified 2-manifold graph representation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 3D人体网格恢复和穿衣人体重建长期被孤立研究，忽视了联合优化的潜力。本文提出MuNet，一个互惠网络，采用2-流形图作为统一表示，端到端地联合优化这两个任务。实验表明，联合学习显著提升了两个任务的精度，尤其是在衣物细节和人体姿态方面。该方法为人体建模提供了更完整的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 3D人体网格恢复和穿衣人体重建长期被孤立研究，忽视了联合优化的潜力。本文提出MuNet，一个互惠网络，采用2-流形图作为统一表示，端到端地联合优化这两个任务。实验表明，联合学习显著提升了两个任务的精度，尤其是在衣物细节和人体姿态方面。该方法为人体建模提供了更完整的解决方案。

quick_skim.title: Stabilizing Streaming Video Geometry via Dynamic Feature Normalization
quick_skim.abstract: Consistent 3D geometry estimation from streaming RGB input is crucial for real-world applications such as autonomous driving, embodied AI, and large-scale reconstruction. While modern monocular geometry foundation models achieve strong single-image accuracy, they exhibit severe temporal inconsistency on continuous input, notably dominated by scale--shift drifting. Through targeted empirical analysis, we trace this instability to its root cause: fluctuations in latent feature statistics, whose mean and variance directly determine the predicted depth's scale and shift. Building on this insight, we introduce Dynamic Feature Normalization (DyFN), a lightweight, causal recurrent module that dynamically and robustly modulates feature statistics to maintain stable geometry over time. We adapt powerful pretrained monocular geometry models for streaming by finetuning only DyFN, a mere 2\% additional parameters, while keeping the backbone frozen, thereby achieving temporal consistency without compromising single-image accuracy. Extensive experiments across four benchmarks show that DyFN effectively eliminates temporal artifacts such as disjointed layering and positional jitter, and achieves state-of-the-art temporal stability, improving over prior streaming methods by up to 14\% and even outperforming heavier non-causal video baselines. Project Page: https://shawlyu.github.io/DyFN
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: stabilizing streaming 3D geometry via feature normalization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过特征归一化稳定流式3D几何
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过特征归一化稳定流式3D几何
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过特征归一化稳定流式3D几何
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DyFN, a causal recurrent module that stabilizes streaming 3D geometry estimation by dynamically normalizing latent features, eliminating scale-shift drift.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 流式3D几何估计存在严重的尺度平移漂移问题，根源在于潜在特征统计量的波动。本文提出动态特征归一化（DyFN），一种轻量级因果循环模块，通过动态鲁棒地调节特征统计量来保持时序一致性。实验表明，DyFN能有效消除漂移，显著提升长序列重建的稳定性，为实时3D应用提供可靠保障。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 流式3D几何估计存在严重的尺度平移漂移问题，根源在于潜在特征统计量的波动。本文提出动态特征归一化（DyFN），一种轻量级因果循环模块，通过动态鲁棒地调节特征统计量来保持时序一致性。实验表明，DyFN能有效消除漂移，显著提升长序列重建的稳定性，为实时3D应用提供可靠保障。

quick_skim.title: R5DGS: Semantic-Aware 4D Gaussian Splatting with Rigid Body Constraints for Efficient Dynamic Scene Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: Reconstructing and predicting dynamic 3D scenes from multi-view videos is a foundational task for robotics, AR/VR, and digital twins. Recent physics-informed Gaussian Splatting methods achieve impressive future frame extrapolation but lack semantic awareness and suffer from large computational overhead. We introduce $\textbf{R5DGS}$, a framework that augments a physics-driven 4D Gaussian representation with compact Identity Encoding vectors, enabling precise Gaussian-to-object association. By constructing an offline CLIP-based object lookup table, we support open-vocabulary text prompting to retrieve and render object-specific Gaussians across arbitrary timestamps and viewpoints. Furthermore, we propose a rigid-body inference constraint that predicts and integrates physical dynamics exclusively for object centroids, propagating motion to associated Gaussians via relative transformations. This optimization yields a 11 FPS speedup during extrapolation without compromising trajectories plausibility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view videos using 4D Gaussian Splatting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用4D高斯泼溅从多视角视频进行动态3D场景重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用4D高斯泼溅从多视角视频进行动态3D场景重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用4D高斯泼溅从多视角视频进行动态3D场景重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: R5DGS augments physics-driven 4D Gaussian Splatting with identity encoding and CLIP-based object lookup for semantic-aware dynamic scene reconstruction, achieving efficient future frame extrapolation and open-vocabulary object rendering.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对动态3D场景重建中缺乏语义感知和计算开销大的问题，提出R5DGS框架，在物理驱动的4D高斯表示中增加身份编码向量，实现高斯与对象的精确关联。通过构建离线CLIP对象查找表，支持开放词汇的文本提示检索和渲染特定物体。实验表明该方法在效率和新视角合成质量上优于现有方法，为数字孪生和AR/VR应用提供新的解决方案。

quick_skim.title: Curve Skeletonization in Continuous domain for Meshes and Point Clouds
quick_skim.abstract: Advancements in 3D curve skeletonization are accelerating progress across a wide range of applications. However, developing robust skeletonization algorithms that capture intricate object details remains challenging. Skeletonization via Local Separators (LS) offers an efficient graph-based approach but suffers from representation inaccuracies due to its discrete nature. To address this, we introduce CSCD, a novel framework for Curve Skeletonization in the Continuous Domain, generalizing LS to manifolds. Specifically, we present two realizations: CSCD-M for meshes and CSCD-PC for point clouds. CSCD-M leverages the intrinsic triangulation of a mesh for resilience to noise and improved topological preservation, while CSCD-PC employs tufted Laplacians for enhanced robustness. To our knowledge, CSCD-M is the first intrinsic method for curve skeletonization. Our results show CSCD-M matches LS performance across diverse meshes and outperforms LS (TOG'21) on benchmarks like Thingi10k dataset. CSCD-PC qualitatively outperforms CoverageAxis++ (Eurographics'24) and EPCS (CAG'23). Finally, we demonstrate the efficacy of CSCD in a few downstream tasks: object classification, shape segmentation, identifying handles, tunnels, and constrictions in objects. Project Website: https://cscd-skel.pages.dev
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: curve skeletonization for meshes and point clouds enabling part decomposition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 曲线骨架化用于网格和点云，支持部件分解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 曲线骨架化用于网格和点云，支持部件分解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 曲线骨架化用于网格和点云，支持部件分解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CSCD extends local separators to continuous domain for robust curve skeletonization of meshes and point clouds, aiding shape analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 3D曲线骨架化对形状分析至关重要，但现有离散方法精度不足。本文提出CSCD，将局部分隔子推广到连续域，针对网格和点云分别设计实现。CSCD-M利用网格三角化提升噪声鲁棒性和拓扑保持，CSCD-PC使用簇状拉普拉斯处理点云。实验显示该方法在多种物体上生成更准确且拓扑一致的骨架，为高层形状分析奠定基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 3D曲线骨架化对形状分析至关重要，但现有离散方法精度不足。本文提出CSCD，将局部分隔子推广到连续域，针对网格和点云分别设计实现。CSCD-M利用网格三角化提升噪声鲁棒性和拓扑保持，CSCD-PC使用簇状拉普拉斯处理点云。实验显示该方法在多种物体上生成更准确且拓扑一致的骨架，为高层形状分析奠定基础。

quick_skim.title: Feedforward 3D Editing Learns from Semantic-Part Transformation
quick_skim.abstract: 3D editing is a fundamental capability for scalable 3D content creation. While image editing has rapidly evolved toward large-scale feedforward generative paradigms, 3D AI generation remains dominated by training-free editing pipelines. A central challenge of feedforward 3D editing lies in the lack of high-quality paired supervision. Editable 3D assets require simultaneous preservation of geometry, multi-view consistency, structural coherence, and localized edit controllability. Existing 3D editing datasets often rely on independently generated assets, image-mediated reconstruction or narrow edit taxonomies, leading to inaccurate localization, weak preservation, blurred edit boundaries, and limited semantic consistency. In this work, we introduce a new perspective: scalable feedforward 3D editing should be learned from semantic-part transformations. Based on this insight, we propose Pxform, a high-quality 3D editing dataset with over 100K consistent before/after editing pairs across seven edit types. Instead of treating objects as unstructured shapes, our pipeline grounds edits directly in semantic 3D parts. Built upon Pxform, we further propose PartFlow, a feedforward 3D editing network that injects source-aware latent control into pretrained 3D generative priors. PartFlow introduces mask-aware velocity preservation and render-space consistency supervision to jointly improve edit fidelity and source preservation, while requiring no 3D edit mask during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that high-quality semantic-part supervision substantially improves scalable 3D editing, enabling PartFlow to achieve state-of-the-art performance on both geometric and appearance editing benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 前馈式3D编辑，利用语义部件变换实现编辑
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 前馈式3D编辑，利用语义部件变换实现编辑
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 前馈式3D编辑，利用语义部件变换实现编辑
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a feedforward 3D editing framework that learns from semantic-part transformation, enabling localized editing with geometry preservation and multi-view consistency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 前馈式3D编辑面临缺乏高质量配对监督的挑战，现有数据集依赖独立生成资产或窄编辑分类。本文提出基于语义部件变换的前馈3D编辑方法，通过学习变换规律，在保持几何和多视图一致性的同时实现局部编辑。实验表明该方法在编辑质量和一致性上优于训练无关管线。

quick_skim.title: SEMAGIC: Learning Semantically Consistent Deformable 3D Representations from In-the-Wild Images
quick_skim.abstract: Learning deformable 3D object models from single-view in-the-wild images has enabled impressive 3D shape reconstruction without supervision. However, it remains unclear whether these models capture the semantic structure required for downstream tasks. We find that existing deformable reconstruction approaches, despite producing visually plausible geometry, yield unstable correspondences across instances and perform poorly on semantic correspondence benchmarks. We introduce SEMAGIC, a framework for learning semantically consistent deformable 3D representations from single-view in-the-wild images. Rather than treating reconstruction as the end goal, SEMAGIC uses deformable modeling as a mechanism to discover category-level correspondences. Each category is represented by a canonical template mesh and a learned deformation field, functioning similarly to an autoencoder that reconstructs instance geometry from image features, enabling vertices to maintain consistent semantic meaning across instances. Semantic consistency is enforced during training through (i) a feature-level consistency loss aligning semantic features between canonical and deformed meshes, and (ii) vertex-index-conditioned deformation that preserves semantic correspondence across instances. By explicitly coupling geometric deformation with semantic alignment, SEMAGIC produces representations that maintain stable part correspondences across intra-category variation. Experiments demonstrate that SEMAGIC improves semantic correspondence of deformable models by +14.7 PCK@0.1 on SPair-71k, establishing deformable models as effective semantic 3D representations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可变形3D重建，强调语义一致性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可变形3D重建，强调语义一致性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可变形3D重建，强调语义一致性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SEMAGIC learns semantically consistent deformable 3D representations from single-view in-the-wild images by using deformable modeling to discover category-level correspondences, improving performance on semantic correspondence benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有可变形3D重建从单视图野生图像中学习，但产生的对应关系不稳定，语义一致性差。本文提出SEMAGIC，以可变形建模为手段发现类别级对应，而非仅以重建为目的。在多个语义对应基准上，SEMAGIC显著提升了跨实例的语义一致性，展示了3D重建中语义结构学习的重要性。
